overview.centralIdea: what Software Design Patterns is — frequently abbreviated as gof

claims.fact: Design patterns gained popularity in computer science after the book Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software was published in 1994 by the so-called Gang of Four , which is frequently abbreviated as GoF.[6] That same year, the first Pattern Languages of Programming Conference was held, and the following year the Portland Pattern Repository was set up for documentation of design patterns.

assessment.fact: Design patterns gained popularity in computer science after the book Design Patterns: Elements of Reusable Object-Oriented Software was published in 1994 by the so-called Gang of Four , which is frequently abbreviated as GoF.[6] That same year, the first Pattern Languages of Programming Conference was held, and the following year the Portland Pattern Repository was set up for documentation of design patterns.

how.mainIdea: can speed up the development process by providing proven development paradigms
how.centralIdea: how Software Design Patterns works — can speed up the development process by providing proven development par

claims.fact: Design patterns can speed up the development process by providing proven development paradigms.[8] Effective software design requires considering issues that may not become apparent until later in the implementation.

claims.fact: Object-oriented design patterns typically show relationships and interactions between classes or objects, without specifying the final application classes or objects that are involved.[citation needed] Patterns that imply mutable state may be unsuited for functional programming languages.

summary.mainIdea: a reusable solution to a commonly needed behavior in software
summary.centralIdea: essential takeaway — a reusable solution to a commonly needed behavior in software

claims.fact: A software design pattern describes a reusable solution to a commonly needed behavior in software.[1] A design pattern is not a rigid structure to be copied directly into source code.
claims.subject: A software design pattern
claims.assertion: a reusable solution to a commonly needed behavior in software

assessment.fact: A software design pattern describes a reusable solution to a commonly needed behavior in software.[1] A design pattern is not a rigid structure to be copied directly into source code.
